items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Temple Church
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* edinburgh lothians
*County:* Midlothian
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 14th century
*Coords:* 51.5132, -0.1104
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temple,_Midlothian
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Temple%20Church%20Midlothian%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/temple-church.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* Update name to Old Temple Kirk , google maps link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/biwFY3CwJ8pGTV1v6 , add source - https://www.templeoldkirk.org/default.asp?page=history , remove wikipedia link as no pages seem to exist 

*Description:*
The Temple Church, a royal peculiar in the Church of England, is a church in the Inner and Middle Temples located between Fleet Street and the River Thames in London, England. It was built by the Knights Templar for their English headquarters in the Temple precinct and consecrated on 10 February 1185 by Patriarch Heraclius of Jerusalem. During the reign of King John (1199–1216) it served as the royal treasury, supported by the role of the Knights Templar as proto-international bankers. It is now jointly owned by the Inner Temple and Middle Temple Inns of Court, bases of the English legal profession. It is famous for being a round church, a common design feature for Knights Templar churches, and for its 13th- and 14th-century stone effigies. It was heavily damaged by German bombing during World War II and has since been greatly restored and rebuilt.

_Listing 4 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Torphichen Preceptory
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* edinburgh lothians
*County:* West Lothian
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 55.93464, -3.6524
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Torphichen_Preceptory
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Torphichen%20Preceptory%20West%20Lothian%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Torphichen_Preceptory_5.jpg/330px-Torphichen_Preceptory_5.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Torphichen Preceptory is a church in the village of Torphichen, West Lothian, Scotland. It comprises the remains of the preceptory (headquarters) of the Knights Hospitaller of the Order of St John of Jerusalem in Scotland. The placename may be Gaelic in origin, e.g., "Tóir Féichín" or Brythonic, e.g., modern Welsh "tref fechan". The name "Tóir Féichín" with its Gaelic reference to the Boundary/Sanctuary stones set one mile around the village is most likely the true meaning of the village and Preceptory name rather than the modern Welsh which has no historical precedent that overrides the true meaning of those Neolithic sanctuary stones.

_Listing 1 of 10_
